title: Serrafalcus cavanillesii

Traditionally, Serrafalcus cavanillesii has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which are responsible for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Serrafalcus cavanillesii is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and infusates, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues.

The genus Serrafalcus is a relatively small and distinct group within the Fabaceae family. Serrafalcus cavanillesii is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe.
